hard.next_step: 6/30/26 : Sharing Zycus overview material and reconnect with Eric to define the initial greenfield scope for Charging NC around SRPM, Intake, CLM, and Spend Analysis. Keeping prospect nurtured by inviting to Horizon. To Follow up in November.
hard.ais_why:
• Charging NC is at Qualified on an SRPM motion with only Merlin scoped and no discovery description recorded.
• The Agentic-platform-discussed flag is not set and there are no pain points or AI sentiment captured.
• Product scope suggests openness to Zycus AI-enabled SRPM, but evidence is too thin for more than exploratory appetite.

north_star_verdict.headline: Brand-new greenfield opportunity (created 2026-06-30) in early Qualified with a 394-day runway to a 2027-07-31 close. An intro call landed ~29 Jun and Eric Rabuse emailed a positive thank-you on 30 Jun, so the buyer is engaged, not silent. The rep is sharing overview material and reconnecting with Eric to scope SRPM, Intake, CLM, and Spend Analysis, with a Horizon invite and a November follow-up planned. Stage-appropriate pace for an early greenfield deal.
north_star_verdict.math: Created 2026-06-30, 6 days old at sweep (2026-07-06). Days to close ~390 (2027-07-31). Time in Qualified: 6 days. A Qualified stage with 13 months to close is stage-appropriate for a greenfield enterprise motion (typical 12-15 month cycle). No slip history. Pace required: discovery depth, demo, and RFI/RFP positioning over the next 60-90 days to progress toward Formal Evaluation; the buyer-side intro call (~29 Jun) and Eric's 30 Jun reply show live early engagement.
evidence: Task 2026-06-30: Eric Rabuse emailed 'Great meeting with you both yesterday. Thanks for your time.' after an intro call ~29 Jun (buyer-side engagement)
Next Step 2026-06-30: share overview material, reconnect with Eric to scope SRPM/Intake/CLM/Spend Analysis, Horizon invite, November follow-up
Products scope defined: iContract, iSupplier, Merlin
No Avoma buyer calls discovered (deal is 6 days old)
Stage Qualified, appropriate for a ~390-day runway

metrics.narrative: No quantified business problem or ROI case exists yet. This deal is 6 days old (created 2026-06-30) and formal discovery has not run. The Next Step (2026-06-30) shows the rep scoping product areas (SRPM, Intake, CLM, Spend Analysis) but no buyer has stated a cost, cycle-time, headcount, or savings figure. Eric Rabuse's 30 Jun thank-you email confirms an intro call happened but carried no metrics. The move is a structured discovery call with Eric to surface the operational pain (supplier risk, contract turnaround, spend visibility) and quantify it before pushing past Qualified.

economic_buyer.narrative: The Economic Buyer is not identified. Eric Rabuse (eric.rabuse@chargingnc.com) is the only named person, appearing as a task/activity contact on the 2026-06-30 email, but no title, role, or budget authority is on record and there are no OpportunityContactRole entries. For a $100K greenfield utilities SRPM deal the budget owner is likely a Director/VP of Procurement, Finance, or Operations. The conversation is too early to infer who controls the commercials, so the move is to ask Eric directly on the discovery call who owns the budget and to request an early introduction.

decision_criteria.narrative: Evaluation criteria are undocumented. The product scope (SRPM, Intake, CLM, Spend Analysis via iSupplier/iContract/Merlin) implies functional interest in supplier risk/performance, contract lifecycle, and spend visibility, but Charging NC has shared no scorecard, must-haves, or RFP/RFI. This is expected at 6 days into Qualified. The discovery call should surface what Charging NC will measure vendors against (ERP integration, supplier onboarding speed, contract automation, Merlin AI capabilities, reporting) and whether a formal RFP is planned or this stays a sole-sourced greenfield build.
sources: No Avoma calls or RFP/RFI documentation

decision_process.narrative: The decision process is unmapped. There are no OpportunityContactRole records and no buyer calls transcribed to read the process from. The rep's Next Step (2026-06-30) references a November follow-up, implying a long, unhurried cycle, but the actual path (discovery, demo, RFI/RFP, shortlist, commercials, EB sign-off) and approval chain are unknown. The intro call (~29 Jun) plus Eric's reply are the only touchpoints. The move is to establish on the discovery call whether Charging NC runs a formal RFP, who participates (procurement, IT, business), and their internal timeline toward the 2027-07-31 target.

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are entirely unknown, which is appropriate for a 6-day-old Qualified deal. There has been no discussion of MSA/Order Form/SOW, legal review, procurement channel, InfoSec, or SI/partner involvement, and Current_Contract_Expiration__c is not populated (a genuine greenfield build with no incumbent contract to displace). As this progresses toward Shortlisted/Vendor Selected, surface whether Charging NC needs a new MSA, requires InfoSec review for Merlin AI, and what its procurement approval flow looks like, so late-cycle paperwork does not become a bottleneck.

identify_pain.narrative: The specific operational or financial pain has not been articulated by the buyer. The scoped modules (SRPM, Intake, CLM, Spend Analysis) point to likely process gaps in supplier risk/performance management, contract handling, and spend visibility at a utilities company, but no one at Charging NC has stated the pain in their own words. Eric Rabuse's 30 Jun 'great meeting' email confirms interest without naming a problem. The move is to have the discovery call uncover and document the pain in the buyer's language, quantify it, and identify the pain owner who will champion a solution.

champion.narrative: No champion is confirmed. Eric Rabuse is the sole named contact and is at least responsive and warm, having emailed 'Great meeting with you both yesterday. Thanks for your time.' on 2026-06-30 after the intro call, which is a genuine positive early signal. However his role, seniority, and access to any Economic Buyer or Decision Maker are unknown, and there is no OpportunityContactRole marking him a champion. The move is to assess Eric's influence on the discovery call and, if he is mid-level or a coach, cultivate a senior procurement or operations sponsor who can drive internal momentum.

competition.narrative: The competitive picture is unknown. Competitors__c is blank, no buyer calls have occurred, and neither the Next Step nor any task names a rival or incumbent. This is a greenfield build (no Current_Contract_Expiration__c, no incumbent to replace), so it may be sole-sourced, but that is unconfirmed. The discovery call must establish whether Charging NC is evaluating other SRPM/CLM/spend vendors or running a formal RFP. If competitive, the wedge is Merlin's AI-driven supplier intelligence and negotiation automation; if sole-sourced, confirm it explicitly to de-risk a late-cycle competitor entry.
sources: No Next Step or Task mentions of competing vendors

An intro call landed ~29 Jun and Eric Rabuse emailed a positive follow-up on 30 Jun. This is the deal's first sweep, so the movement is the initial qualification and the live buyer-side reply.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: A 6-day-old greenfield SRPM opportunity with one warm-but-unqualified contact (Eric Rabuse) and no discovery yet; the deal advances only once we convert his 30 Jun thank-you into a structured discovery call that surfaces pain and the budget owner.

win_position.text: Early and thin: Charging NC is 6 days into Qualified with no pain quantified, no Economic Buyer mapped, and no competitive picture, so this is exploratory rather than a defensible win position.
The one bright spot is live engagement: Eric Rabuse emailed 'Great meeting with you both yesterday. Thanks for your time.' on 30 Jun after the intro call, so the buyer is responsive, not silent.
Single-threaded on Eric Rabuse, whose role and budget authority are unknown, so the deal rests on one unqualified contact until we multi-thread.
No competitive intel exists on a greenfield build, so a rival could enter late unless we confirm sole-source or the buyer's evaluation plan on discovery.

deal_momentum.text: Intro call held ~29 Jun and answered by Eric's positive 30 Jun email, giving the deal a genuine two-way start.
Only a November follow-up is planned against a 2027-07-31 close, so momentum will stall unless a discovery call is booked in the next two weeks.

customer_commitment.text: Charging NC has committed to nothing concrete yet: no dates, no RFP, no next meeting requested beyond the rep's own November plan (SF Next Step 2026-06-30).
Eric Rabuse's proactive thank-you email signals willingness to keep talking, a foothold to convert into a scoped next step.

commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 0 is anchored on the stage win (5), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 54, and dragged by risk 0. Read is Early Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Early Read: 2 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (stage integrity, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.
